QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_18

question.text: A Disclosed Quantity (DQ) order allows a trader to:

answer.text: Show only a portion of the total order quantity to the market at a time
Hide the security name from other market participants

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A DQ order displays only a portion of the total order to the market. For example, an order of 1,000 with DQ of 200 shows only 200 at a time. After 200 is traded, the next 200 is automatically released, and so on until the full order is executed. **The Trap:** The order quantity is the total, and DQ is the visible portion. The hidden portion doesn't get time priority until it becomes visible. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers DQ orders in derivative trading. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** DQ is used by large traders to avoid market impact — showing a huge order could move the price against them. The exchange sets minimum DQ criteria. Each new DQ release gets a fresh time stamp, so it loses time priority.
